title: Leadership and constituent emails

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Hampshire Right to Know Law, I hereby request the following records:

A copy of all email communications sent and received by the current chair of the town council related to their elected position between Jan 1, 2026 and Jan 31, 2026, including but not limited to emails with constituents. This request is inclusive of all email sent to their official email, and all correspondence related to their elected position sent or received on personal and secondary accounts.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

In some cases, these records may be considered exempt from disclosure because of a privacy interest or some other concern. When considering whether a government record should be released, the courts routinely conduct a balancing test to weigh the public's interest in the records versus the government's interest in non-disclosure. We believe the public interest in these specific records is very high.

Nothing prevents you from making these records available to the public. Both the spirit and letter of the state's Right-to-Know law ensure "the greatest possible public access" to government records and proceedings. We ask that you consider this request carefully and conduct your own balancing test if you are inclined to deny any portion of this request.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen (Muckrock) and Jonathan Van Fleet (Concord Monitor)

title: Accurint / PSDEX Data Sharing Fields (Maplewood Police Department)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Minnesota Government Data Practices Act, I hereby request the following records:

1) Technical Specifications: Any Interface Control Document (ICD) or technical implementation guide that defines the data structure and data elements for the automated export of Records Management System (RMS) or Computer-Aided Dispatch (CAD) data to the "CISC Data Warehouse" or the LexisNexis "PSDEX private container".
2) Data Mapping: Any "Data Translation" guides, field-mapping tables, or schemas used to configure the transfer of local agency data into the LexisNexis Public Safety Data Exchange (PSDEX) or Virtual Crime Center (VCC) platforms.
3) Field Lists: Any record or "Schedule A" line item defining the "Database Interface" capabilities, specifically identifying which categories of information (e.g., Victim PII, Witness Contact Data, Real-Time Jail Bookings) are included in the nightly (or regular) automated feed.

Exclusion Note: We are not seeking URLs, passwords, network IP addresses, encryption keys, or sensitive security information, or contents of any data fields. We are seeking only the field name of the data elements (e.g., "Name," "DOB," "SSN," "Narrative") that have been approved for mass transfer to LexisNexis/PSDEX.

Context: We are trying to answer the question - What data does your agency contribute to LexisNexis/PSDEX? This information may sit with your organization's IT department, and may be stored in knowledge bases, emails, or other folders from when the LexisNexis/PSDEX integration took place.

title: William J. Clinton Presidential Library: WHTV Tapes of December 8th, 1999 meeting between Clinton and Ukrainian President Leonid Kuchma

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: William J. Clinton Presidential Library: WHTV Tapes of December 8th, 1999 meeting between Clinton and Ukrainian President Leonid Kuchma
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am seeking access to specific audiovisual records maintained by the Clinton Presidential Library created by the White House Communications Agency (WHCA) and White House Television (WHTV).

According to previously released copies of Clinton's schedule on Wednesday, December 8th, 1999, President Clinton held a meeting with Ukrainian President Leonid Kuchma in the Oval Office from 10:15 am to 10:45 am. While the schedule for that day notes "STILLS ONLY," photographic evidence confirms the presence of a formal photo opportunity. Standard operating procedure for WHCA during this era typically included a "WHTV Master" recording of such high-level diplomatic head-of-state meetings for archival and internal purposes.

For reference, here's a photograph from that day: 
https://photo.unian.info/photo/1348-leonid-kuchma-and-william-jefferson-bill-clinton

Specifically, I request:

Any WHCA/WHTV video recordings (including raw footage, pool spray footage, b-roll, or “photo opportunity” coverage) of:

The Oval Office meeting between President Clinton and President Kuchma.

Any associated press “spray,” press availability, or staged photo opportunity in the Oval Office or other White House locations on that date.

Any associated shot logs, tape logs, daily production logs, rundowns, or indices created by WHCA/WHTV for December 8, 1999, that reference:

"President Leonid Kuchma"

"Ukraine"

"Oval Office Meeting"

"WHTV Master" or "WHCA Video" logs for the 10:00 am – 11:00 am timeframe.

Format and Delivery:
I request that these records be provided in a digital format. If the video is located and cleared for release, I respectfully request that the Library consider uploading the digitized footage to the "ClintonLibrary42 YouTube channel" to ensure broad public access to this historical record of U.S.-Ukraine relations.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Robert Delaware

title: Untitled

communications.subject: Texas Public Information Act Request: Untitled
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All incident reports, call logs, computer-aided dispatch (CAD) records, and related documentation from January 1, 2020 to the present reflecting calls for service that were identified as, or subsequently determined to be, initiated by a voice-activated device, virtual assistant, or AI-enabled emergency feature, including but not limited to Apple Siri, Amazon Alexa, Google Assistant, Samsung Bixby, or device-based emergency detection features such as Apple crash detection, fall detection, or Emergency SOS.

All incident reports, call logs, and related documentation from January 1, 2020 to the present reflecting calls for service categorized as accidental, unintentional, pocket dial, abandoned, or device-generated, where responding officers noted or documented that the call originated from a smart device or voice-activated assistant.

Any internal policies, directives, training materials, bulletins, or guidance documents related to responding to or handling calls originating from voice-activated devices, virtual assistants, or AI-enabled emergency features on smart devices.

Any communications, including emails, memos, or internal reports, discussing accidental or unintentional 911 calls generated by AI, smart devices or voice assistants.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Hannah Riley

title: Tijuana River Sewage Crisis / San Diego Beach Closures

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Tijuana River Sewage Crisis / San Diego Beach Closures
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

U.S. Environmental Protection Agency
FOIA Office
1200 Pennsylvania Avenue NW
Washington, DC 20460

RE: Freedom of Information Act Request – Tijuana River Sewage Contamination / San Diego Beach Closures

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, 5 U.S.C. § 552, I request copies of the following records:

Any incident reports, briefing materials, environmental assessments, or internal summaries relating to sewage or wastewater contamination originating from Mexico and impacting beaches or waterways in San Diego County, California.

Any records relating to beach closures, water quality advisories, contamination monitoring, public health concerns, or environmental mitigation efforts involving:
Imperial Beach;
Coronado;
Silver Strand;
the Tijuana River; or
the Tijuana River Valley.

Any communications between the EPA and:
the International Boundary and Water Commission (IBWC);
the State of California;
San Diego County;
the City of Imperial Beach;
the United States Navy; or
Mexican governmental authorities
concerning sewage discharge events, wastewater infrastructure failures, beach closure impacts, or operational impacts to military training or coastal activities.

Any records sufficient to show EPA response actions, coordination efforts, funding discussions, mitigation planning, or interagency coordination related to wastewater contamination events affecting Southern California beaches.

Please limit the timeframe of this request to records created between May 1, 2025 and the present.

I request electronic production of responsive records in PDF format via email.

I request a waiver of fees because disclosure of the requested records is in the public interest and concerns environmental contamination, public health, and impacts to public coastal resources and governmental operations.

If fees are expected to exceed $25, please contact me before processing.

If any records are withheld, please provide the specific FOIA exemptions relied upon and release all reasonably segregable non-exempt portions.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

William Fernandes

title: Chelsea Alston, 32, airport rode away on her motorized luggage

communications.subject: Florida Sunshine Law Request: Chelsea Alston, 32, airport rode away on her motorized luggage
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

A woman, barred from boarding her flight because she was intoxicated, cussed out a police officer and [then rode away on her motorized luggage](https://www.clickorlando.com/news/local/2022/02/15/full-video-drunken-woman-on-motorized-suitcase-leads-police-chase-through-orlando-airport-officials-say/). An officer pursued her through the airport—on a bicycle. Chelsea Alston, 32, was later accused of battering the police officer and causing more than $1,000 in damage to his patrol car, court records allege. If convicted, she faces up to five years in prison for each offense.
February 15, 2022
Location: Orlando International Airport, Orlando FL
Agency: Orlando Police Department or Aviation Authority Police

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

communications.subject: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Open Records Request: Fusus logs (Gwinnett Technical College)
communications.communication:
Dear Sir or Madam,

Gwinnett Technical College received your Open Records Request dated May 13, 2026, requesting:

"Audit logs/audit trails documenting each time a department account has accessed live or recorded video footage through the Fusus platforms from 1/1/2024 until the date this request is processed."

In response to your request, the College has determined that while certain Fusus hardware was purchased, the College did not enter into a SaaS agreement or maintain an active platform subscription or ongoing service associated with the Fusus platform.

As a result, the College does not maintain responsive records reflecting departmental access to live or recorded footage through the Fusus platform during the requested timeframe.

Accordingly, there are no responsive records available for production.




[Email Signature]Thank you,
 LaShanta' Cox

title: Work Unit ALPR Coordinator (email metadata)

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: Work Unit ALPR Coordinator (email metadata)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

The below-listed fields of information for all emails to or from the "Work Unit ALPR Coordinator" that is mentioned in https://isp.illinois.gov/StaticFiles/docs/DepartmentDirectives/ENF-051%20DIR.pdf: "The Deputy Director of the Division of Criminal Investigation (DCI) will designate a Statewide ALPR Coordinator, who will coordinate directly with the Division ALPR Liaison."

please limit the scope of this request to all emails sent/received from Jan 1, 2024 through the present date.

This request seeks to simplify the narrowing process for followups to #06347171. Requests for metadata like this have been incredibly helpful in order to reduce the burden on the public body and metadata requests by allowing the public to identify the set of emails that they'd like to request, then request them. This sort of process has been invaluable in FOIA requests within Chicago, particularly during FOIA litigation against the Chicago Police Department where the metadata records has helped us provide good-faith reductions of burden on the public body (despite the public body waiving its ability to claim that the request is unduly burdensome under 5 ILCS 140/3(g) under said-litigation).

It's often that these metadata requests get denied for policy or technical reasons. If your agency believes that it does not have the tooling or technical means to complete this request, please let me know and I can provide vendor-approved tooling to trivially complete this task. Tools that can do this include: excel, powershell, compliance center. These vendor tools have been invaluable to close FOIA litigation in the past and we are happy to provide assistance in using them.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

LPL

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New York Freedom of Information Law, I hereby request the following records:

All voter registration records (including past party affiliations) and primary, general and special election voting histories for Shamsul Haque, dating back to 1995. Shamsul Haque's date of birth is 10/24/1972. Haque is currently registered at an address in Queens County: 32-56 69th Street, Woodside, NY 11377.

Other names for Shamsul Haque may include "Shamsul Hague" or "Shansul Haque". 

I am requesting this voter registration data to be used for an “elections purpose” per New York State Election Law Section 3-103(5). communications.communication:
BOARD OF ELECTIONS IN THE CITY OF NEW YORK Dear Sir/Madam: This is an automatically
generated response to your recent e-mail message. The Board of Elections in the City of New York (Board)
acknowledges receipt of your Freedom of Information Law (FOIL) request. Be advised, FOIL requests are processed in the order in which they
are received. Additionally, Board staff are currently focused on fulfilling the
Board’s statutorily mandated pre and post-election activities for the
April Special Election in the 3rd NYC Council District, including building
and testing the ballot, ensuring poll sites are selected, properly
equipped and staffed, and in compliance with all necessary legal requirements,
conducting nine days of early voting, the rolling canvass, the post-election
canvass, completing all required audits, and the ultimate certification of the
election results. Further, the Board is completing its required activities to
prepare for the 2026 Primary and General Elections, including the
imminent filing period for election documents, corresponding cures,
objections, and subsequent ballot access hearings for the June 2026 Primary
Election. This important statutory function of the Board requires the full
attention of staff to fulfill the Board's legal mandates regarding the
intake and review of filed election documents, processing of objections
filed to specific candidacies, and all related litigation. Based on the foregoing and the estimated time it may take to
search, sort, compile and review the records being requested, it is anticipated
the assembly of any available documents will be completed on or before ninety
days (90) days following the date of this notice. Please note, this timeframe
may be modified depending on changing conditions. Your request will be forwarded to the appropriate staff as soon as
possible. When staff is available, a search for any responsive records will be
conducted and the results will be sent to the Records Access Office. You will
be contacted in writing and advised if any records responsive to your request
are located and, if subject to disclosure, when they will be available for
review. Should you request copies of such records, pursuant to the New
York State Public Officers Law § 87(1)(b)(iii), there is a fee of 25¢ per
photocopy of documents nine inches by fourteen inches or less. For larger
documents, the actual cost of reproduction will be charged in accordance with
Public Officers Law § 87(1)(c). The BOE accepts cash, money order, or a corporate check made
payable to the Board of Elections in the City of New York. Please be prepared
to pay for the reproduction costs, if any, when you pick up the materials. If your request is denied you may appeal this determination to the
Board’s FOIL Appeals Officers. Please see the Board’s “Records Access Policy –
FOIL Rules,” which is located on the Board’s website, for additional
information regarding FOIL appeals. 
 Please note, NYS Election Law § 3-103(5) prohibits using
information derived from voter registration records for non-election purposes.
Any person who knowingly and willfully violates this provision is guilty of a
misdemeanor (NYS Election Law § 17-168). 
 
 Records Access Office of the Board of Elections in the City of New
York

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: North Chicago Sergeant  - School Threat, Investigation
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to the applicable public records laws of Illinois, we respectfully request copies of records relating to any reported threat involving a potential shooting or otherwise at the North Chicago schools involving the alleged inaction by a North Chicago Sergeant Cory Marquart. 

This request includes, but is not limited to:

1. All complaints by the Mayor, North Chicago Chief of Staff, North Chicago Police Chief, or others, citizen complaints, administrative investigations by the North Chicago Police Chief, Chief of Staff, or others and supervisory reviews, or disciplinary investigations concerning:

North Chicago Sergeant Marquart's:

* Handling of the reported threat
* North Chicago Sergeant Marquart's alleged failure to investigate,
* North Chicago Sergeant Marquart's alleged failure to act,
* North Chicago Sergeant's alleged inaccurate statements (lying),
* North Chicago Sergeant Marquart's alleged policy violations related to the incident.

2. Any findings by the Mayor, North Chicago Chief of Staff, Attorneys, and/or the North Chicago Police Chief which includes disciplinary determinations, corrective actions, suspension records, resignation records, retirement records, separation agreements, or settlement agreements related to the North Chicago Sergeant and his conduct.

3. Any records reflecting whether this particular North Chicago Sergeant or any North Chicago Sergeant or any police personnel retired, resigned, was placed on leave, or otherwise separated from employment following this complaint or investigation.

4. Any department policies, procedures, directives, or training materials in effect at the time concerning:

   * school threats,
   * social media threats,
   * active shooter threat assessment,
   * mandatory escalation procedures,
   * or officer reporting obligations.

If any portion of the requested records is withheld, please provide:

* the specific legal basis for the withholding,
* identification of the withheld records,
* and all reasonably segregable non-exempt portions.

If exemptions are asserted relating to juvenile, student, or personally identifying information, I request that exempt information be redacted rather than the entire record withheld where possible.

We request that records be provided electronically.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

AnthonyC1970

title: Positive drug screenings by facility

communications.subject: West Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Positive drug screenings by facility
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the West Virginia Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

A database or spreadsheet of the total number of positive drug screenings by facility at the time of this request and for the last five years. The West Virginia Division of Corrections and Rehabilitation Jail Units Inmate Handbook (https://wvpolicy.org/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/Jail-Handbook-All-Jails-2024.04.08.pdf) describes that each inmate is screened. I am not requesting the individual-level data, but aggregate data by facility. I request this data in a machine-readable database or spreadsheet format such as Excel, CSV, Access or SQL.

I also request all documentation that describes the layout of the database’s tables and fields, and that provides the definitions of any field names, codes, and abbreviations used in the database. This information may be gathered together in a single document like a data dictionary, entity layout, or user's guide. If that is the case, I request access to and copy of said document.

title: FOIA Request: Flock Safety

communications.subject: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: FOIA Request: Flock Safety
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Michigan Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I respectfully request copies of the following records held by Monroe County Sheriff's Office related to the use of automated license plate readers (ALPRs) for the period of May 12, 2024 to May 12, 2026.

Please provide copies of the following records:

1. Contracts with ALPR Vendors. All contracts, agreements, memoranda of understanding, purchase orders, subscription agreements, statements of work, or similar documents between Monroe County Sheriff's Office and any company providing ALPR technology or services, including but not limited to Flock Safety, Vigilant Solutions, Rekor Systems, Motorola Solutions, or any other ALPR vendor. This includes all amendments, renewals, extensions, or addenda effective during the time period of this request.

2. Policies on ALPR Data Access. All policies, standard operating procedures, manuals, directives, guidance documents, and training materials effective or revised during the time period of this request, which describe:
   a. Which personnel (by role, rank, title, or unit) are authorized to access, view, retrieve, query, search, or download ALPR data, whether stored by the vendor or by Monroe County Sheriff's Office.
   b. The procedures for granting, monitoring, suspending, or revoking such access privileges.

3. Policies on ALPR Searches and Alerts. All policies, standard operating procedures, manuals, directives, guidance documents, and training materials effective or revised during the time period of this request, which describe:
   a. The circumstances under which a license plate may be queried or searched in ALPR data, including required justifications or supervisory approvals.
   b. The criteria and procedures for creating, approving, or maintaining alerts, notifications, "hot lists," or watchlist entries associated with specific license plates.

4. Policies on ALPR Search Logs, Access Logs, and Audits. All policies, standard operating procedures, manuals, directives, guidance documents, and training materials effective or revised during the time period of this request, which describe:
   a. The maintenance, retention, or structure of logs documenting ALPR searches, including the identity of the user, date/time of access, and stated purpose.
   b. All internal or external auditing procedures for ALPR access, including any reports, summaries, or findings generated as a result of such audits.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

A
